stem: An engineer takes 25 measurements of body weight using a bathroom scale. The measurements have a mean of 136.6 kg and a standard deviation of 20.4 kg. However, calibration tests reveal that a bathroom scale systematically overestimates the true value by between 2.1% and 4.3%. Estimate the true body weight and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 136.6 kg. After correcting for the 3.2% bias: ≈ 132.4 kg. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.1%-4.3%) and sampling variability: [122.8, 142.0]. Point estimate: 132.4 kg.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
